skill.name: embedding-strategies
skill.description: Select and optimize embedding models for semantic search and RAG applications. Use when choosing embedding models, implementing chunking strategies, or optimizing embedding quality for specific domains.
skill.summary: Guide for selecting and optimizing embedding models for vector search and RAG applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides educational content about embedding strategies for RAG applications. All static findings are false positives: code blocks are markdown documentation examples, URLs are legitimate documentation links, and no cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance behavior is present. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers choose and implement the right embedding models for vector search, providing code templates for OpenAI, Sentence Transformers, and domain-specific pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Compare embedding models by dimensions, token limits, and use cases
Implement embedding pipelines with OpenAI API and local models
Apply chunking strategies for optimal vectorization
Evaluate embedding quality with retrieval metrics
Reduce embedding dimensions with Matryoshka approach
limitations: Does not provide actual API keys or authentication
Does not execute code or make live API calls
Requires external dependencies like OpenAI or sentence-transformers
Does not host or manage vector databases

use_cases.title: Build RAG Applications
use_cases.description: Set up embedding pipelines for retrieval-augmented generation with optimal chunking and model selection

use_cases.title: Compare Embedding Models
use_cases.description: Evaluate different embedding models based on dimensions, cost, and performance for specific domains

use_cases.title: Optimize Vector Search
use_cases.description: Improve semantic search quality through proper preprocessing, chunking, and embedding configuration

prompt_templates.title: Basic Embedding Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to get started with OpenAI embeddings for a RAG application. Include batching and dimension reduction.

prompt_templates.title: Local Embedding Models
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I set up local embedding models using sentence-transformers? Include BGE and E5 models with proper preprocessing.

prompt_templates.title: Chunking Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: What chunking strategy should I use for technical documentation? Include token-based, sentence-based, and semantic approaches.

prompt_templates.title: Quality Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I evaluate the quality of my embeddings for retrieval? Show metrics like precision, recall, MRR, and NDCG.

output_examples.input: How do I choose between text-embedding-3-large and text-embedding-3-small?
output: text-embedding-3-large: 3072 dimensions, 8191 tokens - Best for high accuracy needs
Choose large for complex semantic tasks, small for high-volume applications

output_examples.input: What is the best chunking strategy for code?
output: Use tree-sitter to parse code structure
Chunk by functions, classes, and methods
Include surrounding context for better retrieval

best_practices: Match embedding model to your specific use case (code, prose, multilingual)
Normalize embeddings when using cosine similarity for retrieval
Cache embeddings to avoid recomputing for repeated queries
anti_patterns: Using the wrong model for your domain (e.g., general embeddings for code)
Ignoring token limits which causes truncation and lost information
Mixing embedding models in production which creates incompatible vector spaces

faq.question: What is the best embedding model for RAG?
faq.answer: text-embedding-3-small offers the best balance of cost and quality for most RAG applications. Use text-embedding-3-large when maximum accuracy is required.

faq.question: How do I handle long documents?
faq.answer: Split documents into chunks using token-based or semantic chunking. Aim for 256-512 tokens per chunk with overlap to preserve context.

faq.question: Can I use embeddings offline?
faq.answer: Yes, use sentence-transformers models like BGE or E5. They run locally but require more setup and computational resources.

faq.question: How do I reduce embedding dimensions?
faq.answer: OpenAI's text-embedding-3 models support native dimension reduction. For other models, use PCA or Matryoshka Representation Learning.

faq.question: What metrics should I track?
faq.answer: Track precision@k, recall@k, MRR, and NDCG for retrieval quality. Also monitor latency and cost per embedding.

faq.question: How do I handle multilingual content?
faq.answer: Use multilingual models like multilingual-e5-large or OpenAI's newer models which support 100+ languages natively.
